scenes.title: Scene 1 - arriving at the jungle town

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Tora said it shouldn’t take you that long to get to Komorebi Village, but you feel as if you’ve been walking forever.

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: Did he give me the wrong directions?

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: I hear people! The town has to be behind this bush.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You push your way through the bushes, unable to see through them.

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: Whoa–!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: *thump!*

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: I just fell…but…it feels…soft?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: A bit scared, you slowly open your eyes…

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: …to your face dug straight into a woman's chest.

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: Gah–!

dialogue.speaker: Priti
dialogue.text: Kyaaaa~!!!! Peerrrvvveerrrt!!!

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: W-wait– no– this is a misunderstanding! Miss, I am so sorry, I can explain myself! I’m not a pervert!

dialogue.speaker: Priti
dialogue.text: How dare you put your grubby little face into my chest! I, Priti Leidy, Komorebi Village’s beauty, will not tolerate such disrespect!

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: *sighs* I really need to stop bumping into women this week…

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: Please, Miss, I apologize. Is there any way I can make it up to you?

dialogue.speaker: Priti
dialogue.text: Ohohoho~ Offering to do something for me already? Guess I just have that type of charm! Hmm… let me think about it.

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: *squints* Am I going crazy? I feel like I’ve seen this person before…

dialogue.speaker: Priti
dialogue.text: Are you looking at my chest, you perv!?

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: N-never! I was just – you look kind of familiar, I don’t know.

dialogue.speaker: Priti
dialogue.text: Impossible! You’ll never find someone else with a face like mine!

dialogue.speaker: Priti
dialogue.text: Anyway, I don’t care if you stare at my face all day, I would too. I am Komorebi Village’s beauty, after all. Speaking of the village, we’re having a festival tonight, and I was just tasked to pick some special berries.

dialogue.speaker: Priti
dialogue.text: *sighs* It's located in a cave deep underground though. *sighs* If only there was someone who could help me get them.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Her eyes lock onto yours.

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: U-uh, I’ll get them for you!

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: Please forgive me...

dialogue.speaker: Priti
dialogue.text: PERR~FEECCTTT~!!! Maybe then I’ll forgive you, and not out you as a total pervert!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Before you could even step one foot into Komorebi Village, you’ve already been tasked with an errand, and this time it clearly isn’t for money. You keep a safe distance from Priti as she leads you through the jungle.

dialogue.speaker: Priti
dialogue.text: Stop!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You come to an abrupt halt, and take a look at what lays in front of you – an opening to a large underground cave. In the distance, you spot some glowing, blue berries.

dialogue.speaker: Priti
dialogue.text: See those berries over there? I want you to retrieve those for me, hmm~? Just use the vines to get down. Here’s your basket, fill it as much as you can

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: Guess I have no choice.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You notice the abundant amount of vines growing down the cave. You tighten your grip around the vines and let yourself slide down.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Even though you started your visit here under an unfortunate encounter, you can’t help but laugh to yourself. It was ridiculous, but maybe Tora was right, you just have to let the wind take you where it has to. Moments like this keep you wanting to experience more–

dialogue.speaker: Priti
dialogue.text: What’s taking you so long? Those berries won’t pick themselves!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: –even when there’s a persistent woman in your ear.

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: I just finished filling up the basket! I’m about to head back.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You take the vine to go back up.

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: Huff. Here are the berries you asked for.

dialogue.speaker: Priti
dialogue.text: Ohoho~ I’ll spare you today, and won’t let anyone know how much of a pervert you are!

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: Just today?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Just as you hand over the basket full of berries you see a bird rapidly approaching Priti.

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: Watch out–!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: *whoosh!*

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You watch as Priti’s wig is snatched off of her head.

dialogue.speaker: Priti
dialogue.text: AH!

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: Huh!?

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: What the heck? You’re not a woman! You're the guy who tried selling me items at a ridiculous price! Wuh– What is this all about?

dialogue.speaker: Priti
dialogue.text: Ah…ahaha…

dialogue.speaker: Priti
dialogue.text: There is absolutely no way I’m getting out of this one…

dialogue.speaker: Priti
dialogue.text: I guess I should explain myself, huh?

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: Please do.

dialogue.speaker: Priti
dialogue.text: You were right…about thinking I looked familiar. In fact, I’ve been closer to you long before you thought so.

dialogue.speaker: Priti
dialogue.text: ...Tora Guy?

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: You’re Tora?

dialogue.speaker: Priti
dialogue.text: WAS Tora. That's just one of my many personas. But, what I said to you back there was true and came from my own heart.

dialogue.speaker: Priti
dialogue.text: I know I seem like a total creep right now, but it was never my intention to follow you. I noticed that wherever I was, you were there with me too.

dialogue.speaker: Priti
dialogue.text: There’s just something about you I admire. You have a natural charm.

dialogue.speaker: Priti
dialogue.text: I’m typically what you would call a con artist, aha. It’s just– *sighs* I can’t bring myself to be myself around others…yet, there you were in front of me, freely being yourself. In fact, I’m not even fully sure of who I am.

dialogue.speaker: Priti
dialogue.text: I couldn’t help but be curious, so I may have set it up so that we would bump into each other. I know that kind of contradicts the whole concept of meeting new people, but - I was hoping I could join you along your journey..?

dialogue.speaker: Priti
dialogue.text: Maybe then, I’ll discover who I really am.

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: ...I think I'm just going to go with my gut here. He hasn't caused me any real harm.

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: I’d love to have you around. Your words felt sincere to me. I enjoyed every second I spent with you, so I don’t mind spending much more.

dialogue.speaker: Priti
dialogue.text: Wow, is this really happening?

dialogue.speaker: Priti
dialogue.text: In that case… would you like to attend the lantern festival with me tonight?

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: Of course! By the way, what is your actual name?

dialogue.speaker: Priti
dialogue.text: You can call me Conway.

dialogue.speaker: Priti
dialogue.text: Also, just so you know, if I actually was a local here I think I’d still win the title of Komorebi Village’s Beauty.

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: Please don’t make me regret my decision…

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You and Conway make your way back up to Komorebi Village and follow the river stream down, leading to a huge lake. Your eyes glimmer as you watch the lanterns float away into the sky hoping to touch the stars.

dialogue.speaker: Conway
dialogue.text: Wait right here.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You stand on the sidelines for a minute. After waiting for a good moment, you see Conway struggling to row his boat over to you.

dialogue.speaker: Conway
dialogue.text: Row, darn it!

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: …I can’t believe I wasn’t suspicious of you more. You would think a tour guide who has been working in Lake Ryoka for a while would be a little more active.

dialogue.speaker: Conway
dialogue.text: Hey, hey, no shaming now. You have to admit my disguises are pretty~ impressive.

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: I guess I did fall for them...

dialogue.speaker: Conway
dialogue.text: Are you sure it wasn’t me you were falling for?

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: Why— you…

dialogue.speaker: Conway
dialogue.text: Haha, alright I’ll stop with the teasing... for now.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Conway hands you your own lantern and a marker.

dialogue.speaker: Conway
dialogue.text: Take this marker and write what you would wish for on this lantern. Once you’re done I’ll help you light it up.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: *scritch scritch*

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: Done. Aren’t you going to write anything?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He gives you a warm smile.

dialogue.speaker: Conway
dialogue.text: My wish already came true.

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: Ugh, what a flirt…maybe I should tease him back.

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: You have a charm yourself, y’know?

dialogue.speaker: Conway
dialogue.text: Really? What is it?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Conway puts his hands around yours which was grasping the lantern.

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: Me. Duh.

dialogue.speaker: Conway
dialogue.text: Ohoho~ Are you really going to compete with who’s more full of themselves?

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: I don’t mind a little competition.

dialogue.speaker: Conway
dialogue.text: You said you were my charm, huh? Guess you belong to me now *winks*.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: At this time, you let go of your lantern.

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: Looks like my wish already took effect, huh?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Conway’s face turns a little red before you two suddenly start laughing together. The both of you watch your lanterns float next to one another. It was one of those moments where you wished you could just pause.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: As you watch longer, the flames seem to flicker, and the lanterns struggle to keep afloat as they bump into each other. Conway starts rowing the boat to keep up with our lanterns.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Pondering, you look down at the ground.

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: Conway, this is really nice of you to invite me out, but something still seems to be bothering me.

dialogue.speaker: Conway
dialogue.text: What’s on your mind? I’m all ears.

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: I feel like…everything’s going too fast.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Conway slows down the boat. The wind seemed to have stopped for just a moment. Our lanterns had begun to float downwards. He seems unable to make eye contact with you.

dialogue.speaker: Conway
dialogue.text: We did start a little…weird, and all of this is so sudden—

dialogue.speaker: Conway
dialogue.text: I guess my methods of showing my intentions were…not ideal.

dialogue.speaker: Conway
dialogue.text: I figure whatever comes out of my mouth can sound dishonest if you think about what I do for a living.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: His eyes gaze towards your lanterns and you follow. They had landed on the surface of the water ever so lightly. Conway gradually rowed the boat towards the lanterns. They were still flickering but struggling to stay aflame.

dialogue.speaker: Conway
dialogue.text: Trust…

dialogue.speaker: Conway
dialogue.text: It’s one of those concepts that is easy to understand but you never quite know if it’s ever there.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He stops the boat next to the two lanterns, slowly retrieving them out of the water

dialogue.speaker: Conway
dialogue.text: One’s flame may shine brightly while the other’s flame flickers, unable to withstand the wind, eventually extinguishing.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He lifts his own lantern up and inspects it.

dialogue.speaker: Conway
dialogue.text: Trust can easily be lost.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He gently starts blowing the candle.

dialogue.speaker: Conway
dialogue.text: But like a flame…

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The candle starts to light up. He gives you a faint smile.

dialogue.speaker: Conway
dialogue.text: …Slowly but surely, it can be found and shine bright again.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He picks up your lantern next and hands it over to you. It had extinguished.

dialogue.speaker: Conway
dialogue.text: I know first impressions weren’t great. The way I do things isn’t trustworthy.

dialogue.speaker: Conway
dialogue.text: But I want to learn how to trust, for others to trust me, and to achieve that I would have to trust myself first.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He looks into your eyes.

dialogue.speaker: Conway
dialogue.text: I know the idea of trusting me will be hard, but I’m willing to change—

dialogue.speaker: Conway
dialogue.text: You make me want to change.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He moves his eyes towards your lantern.

dialogue.speaker: Conway
dialogue.text: If it’s alright, I’d like to start over from the beginning again.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You take a moment to think. You stare blankly at your lantern which had blown out a while ago, with some smoke still lingering from the candle. You felt conflicted but you eventually answered him.

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: I want to trust you, Conway, and it will take me some time, but I’m willing to give you a chance and spend that time with you.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: A small gust of wind suddenly blows towards your direction and lights up your lantern. You look at the candle momentarily before looking back at Conway with a faint smile.

dialogue.speaker: Player
dialogue.text: I want to find that light, and not only for it to shine bright, but for it to shine brighter.

dialogue.speaker: Conway
dialogue.text: ...Thank you.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You watch as the flame fully reignites and gently guide the lantern back into the sky. Conway does the same. The two of your lanterns dance together in the air. Although there was a flicker here and there, they both finally had a chance to be guided by the wind.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You gave Conway a chance to redeem himself. Will he keep his word?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The end. Thank you for playing our game!
